name: Roca Foradada y Ermita de Sant Mateu del Bosc — circular por la encina de de la Molinera
region_intro: Hiking around Vallromanes offers access to diverse landscapes within the Vallès Oriental region, situated between the Montseny, Montnegre i el Corredor, and Serralada Litoral Natural Parks. The terrain transitions from flatter valleys to more rugged mountainous areas, featuring Mediterranean forests dominated by oak and pine trees. Hikers can explore mixed woods, riverside vegetation, and areas with vineyards and historical farmhouses. The region provides a network of trails suitable for various fitness levels. Best hiking trails around Vallromanes The most popular hiking route is GR 92: Turó d'en Galzeran Loop, an easy 3.9 miles (6.3 km) trail that takes 1 hour 51 minutes to complete. This route offers panoramic views of Barcelona, Montserrat, and the Mediterranean Sea. Another top favourite among local hikers is Roca Foradada y Ermita de Sant Mateu del Bosc — circular por la encina de de la Molinera, a moderate 7.2 miles (11.6 km) path. This trail passes by the unique Roca Foradada geological formation and the historic Ermita de Sant Mateu del Bosc. Local hikers also love the Mirador de la Cornisa, Sagrat Cor y refugio de la Ferreria del Vedat — circular, a 7.8 miles (12.5 km) trail leading through mixed woods and offering viewpoints, often completed in about 3 hours 53 minutes. Hiking around Vallromanes is defined by Mediterranean forests, varied valleys, and mountainous areas, often featuring historical elements. The extensive network offers options for different ability levels, from easy walks to more demanding routes. The routes in Vallromanes are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.7 stars from over 50 reviews. More than 270 hikers have used komoot to explore Vallromanes's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many hiking trails are available around Vallromanes?
A: Vallromanes offers a wide selection of hiking opportunities, with over 30 routes recorded on komoot. These trails cater to various fitness levels, including 15 easy, 17 moderate, and 1 difficult route, ensuring there's something for everyone.

Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on hikes in Vallromanes?
A: The terrain around Vallromanes is quite varied, transitioning from flatter valleys to more rugged mountainous areas. You'll hike through Mediterranean forests dominated by oak and pine trees, discover riverside vegetation, and pass by vineyards and historical farmhouses. The region is nestled between the Montseny, Montnegre i el Corredor, and Serralada Litoral Natural Parks, offering diverse ecosystems.

Q: Are there any circular hiking routes in Vallromanes?
A: Yes, many of the trails in Vallromanes are circular, offering convenient starting and ending points. Popular circular options include the moderate &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1350821133/roca-foradada-y-ermita-de-sant-mateu-del-bosc-circular-por-la-encina-de-de-la-molinera" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Roca Foradada y Ermita de Sant Mateu del Bosc — circular&lt;/a&gt; and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1350830710/mirador-de-la-cornisa-sagrat-cor-y-refugio-de-la-ferreria-del-vedat-circular" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Mirador de la Cornisa, Sagrat Cor y refugio de la Ferreria del Vedat — circular&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: What natural features or landmarks can I see while hiking in Vallromanes?
A: Hikers can discover several interesting natural features and historical landmarks. A notable natural formation is the unique "Roca Foradada," a rock with an almost perfectly circular opening. You can also visit the historic &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1350821133/roca-foradada-y-ermita-de-sant-mateu-del-bosc-circular-por-la-encina-de-de-la-molinera" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Ermita de Sant Mateu del Bosc&lt;/a&gt;, documented since 993 AD. Additionally, the region features ancient farmhouses, old paths, and the remains of the medieval Montornès or Sant Miquel de Montornès castle.

Q: Are there any viewpoints with panoramic scenery along the trails?
A: Absolutely. Many trails in Vallromanes offer breathtaking panoramic views. For instance, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1324929393/gr-92-turo-d-en-galzeran-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;GR 92: Turó d'en Galzeran Loop&lt;/a&gt; provides vistas of Barcelona, Montserrat, and the Mediterranean Sea. From higher points, such as near the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3174771" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;La Coscollada Observation Tower&lt;/a&gt;, you can enjoy beautiful sea views and the Maresme area.

Q: What do other hikers say about the trails in Vallromanes?
A: The trails in Vallromanes are highly regarded by the komoot community, with an average rating of 4.7 stars from over 50 reviews. Hikers often praise the diverse landscapes, the well-maintained paths, and the stunning panoramic views of the sea and mountains.

Q: Are there any historical sites or ancient ruins to explore?
A: Yes, the area is rich in history. Along the trails, you can find ancient farmhouses, old paths, and the remains of the medieval Montornès or Sant Miquel de Montornès castle. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1350821133/roca-foradada-y-ermita-de-sant-mateu-del-bosc-circular-por-la-encina-de-de-la-molinera" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Ermita de Sant Mateu del Bosc&lt;/a&gt; is another significant historical point of interest, with records dating back to 993 AD. You can also explore the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/427891" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Puig Castellar Iberian Settlement&lt;/a&gt; or the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2830271" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Iberian Site of Les Maleses&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: Are there any picnic or rest areas along the hiking routes?
A: Yes, for those looking to take a break, areas like La Font del Grill, located within the Serralada Litoral, offer rustic springs and picnic facilities. These spots provide ideal places for relaxation and enjoying the natural surroundings during your hike.

Q: What is the best time of year to go hiking in Vallromanes?
A: Vallromanes is an excellent destination for hiking throughout much of the year. The Mediterranean climate generally offers pleasant conditions. Spring and autumn are particularly ideal, with mild temperatures and vibrant natural colors. Even in winter, many trails remain accessible and enjoyable, though it's always wise to check local weather conditions.

Q: Are there any easy hiking trails suitable for beginners or families?
A: Yes, Vallromanes offers several easy trails perfect for beginners or families. An excellent option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1324929393/gr-92-turo-d-en-galzeran-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;GR 92: Turó d'en Galzeran Loop&lt;/a&gt;, an easy 6.3 km route that takes less than two hours to complete and offers beautiful panoramic views. Another easy choice is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/31580882" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Can Gurguí Spring loop from Santa Fe&lt;/a&gt;, which is about 5.4 km long.

Q: What kind of wildlife might I encounter while hiking?
A: The diverse ecosystems of the natural parks surrounding Vallromanes support various wildlife. While specific sightings are never guaranteed, you might spot common Mediterranean birds, small mammals, and various insects. The mixed woods and riverside vegetation provide habitats for a range of species.
tips: The Roca Foradada (or Roca Foradada de Can Nadal) is the largest of all the perforated rocks in the Serralada Litoral Park, specifically in the area of Vilanova del Vallès (Vallès Oriental).

This specimen of the genus Quercus suber, evergreen, centenary, of the Fagaceae family, very similar to an oak, has a robust appearance, measures about 13 m high, by 3.80 m trunk vault, by 2.70 m of cannon flight and 18 meters of vertical head.

It is a very simple Romanesque-style hermitage, with a single nave covered with a barrel vault, with a semicircular apse, which has a double-flow window. The nave is very short, which suggests that it was shortened during the restoration,

We must highlight the arrangement of the belfry bell tower, perpendicular to the façade, for its originality. Next to the chapel is the hermit's house.

In the 15th century it was known as San Mateo del Bosque and was property of the Priory of Sant Miquel del Fai.

The chapel has been documented since the year 993. In a pastoral visit from 1498 it is recorded that it is in a ruinous state but it had to be restored shortly after since in the 16th century it had worship again.

In the 16th century restoration, the nave had to be cut off, now quite short in relation to the apse, and with a very simple façade.

On Saint Matthew's Day, its traditional pilgrimage is celebrated.

From the hermitage you can enjoy good views of the Maresme, an area frequented by several hiking and mountain biking routes.

The Can Gurguí Fountain is located in the Parc de la Serralada Litoral, which always flows, little or a lot, with water from the Can Gurguí mine.

   Around the fountain there are stone benches and a round table. For the gloomy surroundings, the freshness of the water and the ease of access, it is one of the most visited sources in the Parc de la Serralada Litoral. In front of the fountain and on the other side of the track, there are a couple of poplars and a lush stream full of plane trees. Behind, there is a thick oak forest. Nearby is Can Gurguí, a former convent and now a restaurant.

  A stone front from 1899 supports the sandstone margin and supports the metal door of the mouth of the mine. The wide spout comes out from under the door and the water that flows is considered sanitary to drink.

waypoints.name: Hermitage of Sant Mateu del Bosc
